vector_metrics.basis: S_t reprojected from MODEL_WEIGHTS (fatigue+tactical+psychology); L_t mu_bias from DC-lambda residuals, sigma_chaos from weather+referee

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.note: The most likely flow — CAI's consensus across form, tactics and momentum.

scenarios.label: Upside · Argentina click
scenarios.note: Argentina's current form holds and they convert their chances — the tie opens up and they pull clear.

scenarios.label: Downside · Switzerland frustrates
scenarios.note: A flat, low-margin night — Switzerland sit in and the tie tightens, live to swing on extra time or penalties.

Argentina: No glaring weakness — a balanced side on current form.
Switzerland: Blunter in the final third — fewer clear chances created.

match_flow.text: Attacking substitutions as both benches go for it

risk_factors: Model confidence is modest (52/100) — wide outcome spread.

reasons: Argentina carries the sharper attack (3.50 vs 1.34 xG/match).
Argentina grades higher on form & big-match composure (33% vs 30%).
Edge from the spot: Argentina convert ~77% vs Switzerland ~75% — decisive if it goes to pens.
explainability.why_penalties: A shootout is unlikely (12%) — one side should settle it inside 120'.
explainability.what_could_change: An early goal swings momentum; an injury to Argentina's key creator, or Switzerland stealing a set-piece, flips it.

disclaimer: This platform is an AI-driven probability simulation built for entertainment and analytics purposes. Not betting advice.
